IMPORTANT: Please make sure to include all Full-Time, Part-Time and Contract workers under your supervision, even if they were not under your direct employment.\n
Please enter the Average Number of Employees your company had during the given year.

Notifiable incident means: (a) the death of a person; or (b) a serious injury or illness of a person; or (c) a dangerous incident. All incidents that were notifiable as defined in Part 3, Section 25 of the Model Work Health and Safety Bill Recordable incidents are all injuries that occur which you do not need to notify to the authorities eg medical treatment injuries. Please only report each incident once based upon the most severe outcome.
Were there any notifiable/recordable incidents for the given year?

Include hours worked by salaried, hourly, part-time and workers including temporary help services workers under your supervision, even if they were not under your direct employment.\nDo not include vacation, sick leave, holidays, or any other non-work time, even if employees were paid for it. If your establishment keeps records of only the hours paid or if you have employees who are not paid by the hour, please estimate the hours that the employees actually worked. If this number isn't available, an estimate is still needed.
Please enter the total number of hours worked by all employees during the given year. Please round your answer to the next highest whole number.
Model Work Health and Safety (WHS) Act – November 2023 Consolidated Version\n\n
Please enter the number of notifiable incidents, resulting in a fatality, for the given year:
Those occurrences that resulted in a permanent disability or time lost from work of one day/shift or more.
Please enter the number of notifiable incidents (excluding fatalities) that resulted in time lost from work for the given year.
Those incidents that resulted in a disability where the individual was unable to follow their ordinary occupation but instead performed alternate or light duty; and/or put on restricted work/hours.
Please enter the number of notifiable incidents for the given year that resulted in restricted work of an employee.
Any incidents that occurred at the workplace which, although not resulting in any injury or disease, had the potential to do so. Defined in Part 3, Section 37
Please enter the number of dangerous occurrences for the given year.

The total number of complete working days or shifts where an individual performed alternate/light duty (or put on restricted work/hours) as a result of the restricted work injury/diseases. Time for part-time workers is calculated as for full-time workers irrespective of the number of hours usually worked each day or shift.
Please enter the total number of days lost from work for all notifiable incident(s) (excluding fatalities) for the given year.

Medical treatment injuries are work-related injuries or illnesses that required treatment by a registered medical practitioner beyond first aid, but did not result in:

* Any lost work time (no full shift/day lost after the day of injury), and
* Any restricted or modified duties.

Include:
Cases requiring stitches, prescription medication, x-rays, diagnostic procedures, or other medical interventions beyond first aid.

Do NOT include:
* First-aid-only cases (e.g., ice packs, bandages, cleaning minor wounds).

* Injuries that resulted in restricted duties (report these separately).

* Injuries that resulted in lost time (report as lost-time incidents).

* Notifiable incidents under WHS laws.

Record all applicable cases, even if they were not reportable to a regulator.
Please enter the number of medical treatment injuries (beyond first aid) for the given year that did not result in restricted work or lost time of an employee
Model Work Health and Safety (WHS) Act – November 2023 Consolidated Version

### High Work Hours:

Are your hours and/or employee count entered correctly? The total number of hours reported are considerably *HIGHER* than the average annual hours typically worked by full-time employees.

Please validate that you have entered your correct employee count and/or total hours. If correct, provide a brief explanation for approval (this will be visible to your client connections). Otherwise, please correct your answers.

### Low Work Hours:

Are your hours and/or employee count entered correctly? The total number of hours reported are considerably *LOWER* than the average annual hours typically worked by full-time and part-time employees.

Please validate that you have entered your correct employee count and/or total hours. If correct, provide a brief explanation for approval (this will be visible to your client connections). Otherwise, please correct your answers.

These recordable are work-related injuries that cause an employee to miss at least one full shift or day, but do not meet the criteria for notifiable incidents. Do not include any cases already reported as notifiable; only enter non-notifiable lost time incidents for the year.
Please enter the number of recordable incidents for the given year that resulted in time lost from work for the given year.

These recordable incidents are injuries that result in an employee being assigned restricted or modified duties, but are not notifiable to the authorities. Do not include any restricted work incidents that were reported as notifiable incidents. Only count non-notifiable restricted duty cases for the year.
Please enter the number of recordable incidents for the given year that resulted in restricted work of an employee.
